title: leucaena pallida
intro: Leucaena pallida, also known as the white leadtree or silver leaf, is a small tree or shrub native to the Americas, belonging to the legume family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucaena pallida include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
